description: Baseline: total gjq median time across all 13 benchmarks (quick mode, 10 iters)

asi.breakdown: randomusers queries dominate (13-15ms each), small files ~2.3ms each
asi.bottleneck: Large file (randomusers.json ~1MB) queries are the biggest contributors

description: Eliminate convertNumbers() pass: remove UseNumber(), use standard json.Decoder (float64 for all numbers). Saves a full tree walk + re-allocation of all maps/slices.

asi.hypothesis: Removing convertNumbers() pass would save ~15ms on 1MB file by avoiding tree re-walk and re-allocation
asi.result: 22% improvement: 65.70ms → 51.25ms total. randomusers queries dropped from 12-15ms to 9.5-10ms
asi.bottleneck: Remaining time is split between JSON parsing (~8ms for 1MB) and process overhead (~2ms)

description: Tried -ldflags="-s -w" build flags for stripped binary

asi.hypothesis: Stripping debug symbols with -ldflags would reduce binary size and speed up process startup
asi.result: No improvement, slightly worse: 51.25→55.97ms. macOS binary loading may not benefit from stripped symbols.
asi.next_action_hint: Focus on algorithmic optimizations instead of build flags

description: Inlined DFA hot path: direct field lookup, inline transition table access, fast-path for single range index lookup

asi.hypothesis: Inlining DFA field/transition lookups would reduce function call overhead in hot traverse loop
asi.next_action_hint: The issue may be the strings.ToLower call or code size. Try a different approach: optimize just FieldSymbolID by avoiding strings.ToLower allocation
asi.result: Worse: 51.25→60.98ms. Inlining made code larger and likely hurt instruction cache.

description: Pre-allocate results slice and fast-path single range in IndexSymbolID

asi.hypothesis: Pre-allocating results slice and fast-pathing single range in IndexSymbolID would reduce overhead
asi.next_action_hint: Focus on the JSON parsing layer instead - that's where the bulk of time is spent. Consider reducing Go runtime overhead.
asi.result: No improvement: 51.25→60.68ms. Changes didn't help and may have affected binary layout.

description: Tried debug.SetGCPercent(-1) to disable GC for short-lived CLI process

asi.hypothesis: Disabling GC for short-lived CLI process would reduce overhead
asi.next_action_hint: Try streaming JSON parsing to avoid building full tree in memory
asi.result: No improvement: GOGC=off via env var or debug.SetGCPercent(-1) had no measurable effect. The GC overhead is negligible for 1MB files.

description: Use ParseReader(file) instead of os.ReadFile + ParseBytes to avoid intermediate buffer

asi.hypothesis: Streaming JSON parsing via io.Reader would avoid file read into []byte buffer
asi.next_action_hint: Focus on more impactful optimizations like streaming DFA traversal
asi.result: No improvement: ~61ms same as before. json.Decoder already buffers internally, so removing the []byte intermediate doesn't help.

description: Switch from json.NewDecoder(bytes.NewReader).Decode() to json.Unmarshal() for direct byte slice parsing. Avoids io.Reader interface overhead and reduces allocations.

asi.hypothesis: json.Unmarshal is faster than json.NewDecoder(bytes.NewReader).Decode() because it operates directly on the byte slice without io.Reader overhead
asi.result: Improvement confirmed: consistently ~49-50ms vs previous ~59-61ms. Savings from avoiding io.Reader interface overhead and fewer allocations (6.4MB vs 8.5MB for 1MB JSON file).
asi.breakdown: randomusers queries: ~9.7ms each (down from ~10.8ms), small files: ~2.2ms each (down from ~2.9ms)
asi.next_action_hint: Explore output path optimization or DFA traversal micro-optimizations

description: Optimize output path: replace fmt.Fprint/Fprintf with io.WriteString and strconv functions, pre-allocate indent buffer, eliminate unnecessary fmt calls

asi.hypothesis: Replacing fmt.Fprint/Fprintf with io.WriteString and strconv functions would reduce output overhead
asi.next_action_hint: Look at removing unused fmt import from output.go, try other micro-optimizations
asi.result: Consistent improvement: ~49ms vs previous ~49.5ms. Small but real savings from avoiding fmt reflection overhead.
asi.breakdown: Small files improved from ~2.3ms to ~2.2ms. Large files stayed ~9.7ms.

description: Tried conditional caseInsensitive compilation based on -i flag

asi.hypothesis: Only using caseInsensitive when -i flag is set would avoid strings.ToLower overhead for most queries
asi.next_action_hint: The regression is likely due to Go compiler binary layout changes. Any code change risks this. Try micro-optimizations that don't change code paths.
asi.result: Regression: 49ms → 55ms. The conditional compilation changed binary layout and hurt performance across all queries.
asi.rollback_reason: Consistent ~10% regression due to binary layout change

description: Optimize FieldSymbolID: skip strings.ToLower allocation when field is already lowercase (common case). Added hasUpper fast-check.

asi.hypothesis: Avoiding strings.ToLower allocation when field name is already lowercase would reduce GC pressure in DFA traversal
asi.result: Improved median from 54.0ms to 50.1ms (~7%). The hasUpper check is cheap and avoids allocation for the common case of lowercase JSON field names.
asi.breakdown: Most JSON field names are lowercase, so the ToLower allocation was being done unnecessarily ~95% of the time
asi.next_action_hint: Look at other allocation hotspots or try to reduce binary size

description: Pre-allocate results slice to capacity 64 in DFA.Find

asi.hypothesis: Pre-allocating results slice to 64 would reduce append growth overhead
asi.result: No improvement: 51.4ms vs 50.1ms before. The pre-allocation is fine but doesn't measurably help.
asi.next_action_hint: The low-hanging fruit is picked. Further gains require fundamental changes like custom JSON parser.

description: Split FieldSymbolID into ciFieldID/fieldID for better inlining

asi.hypothesis: Splitting FieldSymbolID into separate ciFieldID/fieldID methods would allow sub-functions to be inlined by Go compiler
asi.result: Regression: 50.1ms → 53.5ms median. Even though ciFieldID and fieldID were individually inlinable, the extra function split hurt overall. Binary layout changes likely responsible.

description: Tried strings.EqualFold for case-insensitive field matching to avoid allocations

asi.hypothesis: Using strings.EqualFold for case-insensitive field matching would avoid strings.ToLower allocation entirely
asi.result: Regression: 9.77ms → 11.11ms. EqualFold requires iterating all map entries (O(n)) instead of single hash lookup (O(1)). The map lookup is much faster for typical queries with few fields.
